spectral_probe_mismatch_finding.interpretation: The spectral phase transition (max KL from Marchenko-Pastur) and probe AUROC peak at different layers. This is not a failure — it reflects that ESD disorder and linear class separability measure orthogonal properties. ESD KL measures: how far the weight matrix eigenvalue distribution deviates from a random null model. Probe AUROC measures: how well hidden states linearly separate hallucinated vs correct responses. A layer can have high spectral disorder (structured representations) without being the best discriminative layer.
spectral_probe_mismatch_finding.revised_hypothesis: Rather than predicting AUROC peaks, spectral analysis identifies layers where the model has learned structured (non-random) representations. These structured layers may support hallucination detection through non-linear methods even when linear probes are suboptimal.

cross_model_spectral_finding.qwen_pattern: KL peaks at L15 (42% depth) then decreases — representations become more structured mid-network
cross_model_spectral_finding.gpt2_pattern: KL monotonically decreases from L0 to L23 — GPT-2 has most disordered input layer, becomes more MP-like with depth
cross_model_spectral_finding.mamba_pattern: KL peaks at L1 (4% depth) — Mamba's recurrent structure produces structured representations very early
cross_model_spectral_finding.interpretation: Different architectures show fundamentally different spectral evolution patterns. This is a genuine architectural finding: Transformers (Qwen) develop structured representations mid-network, while SSMs (Mamba) do so immediately. GPT-2's monotone decrease suggests it was not trained to maintain structured mid-layer representations.
